title: CVE-2017-1000163 regression — reject /\nevil.com bypass
prompt:
Phoenix had an open-redirect vulnerability (CVE-2017-1000163) where a URL like `/\nevil.com` would pass a `String.starts_with?(url, "/")` check but be treated as external by Chrome and Firefox. Consider this defensive code:

```elixir
def safe_return_to(url) do
  if is_binary(url) and String.starts_with?(url, "/") and not String.starts_with?(url, "//") do
    url
  else
    "/"
  end
end
```

This catches the obvious `//evil.com` protocol-relative case but is still defeated by `/\nevil.com` (newline bypass — the exact CVE-2017-1000163 case) and by `/%2F%2Fevil.com` (URL-encoded bypass). Rewrite the function to reject ANY URL containing: (1) newline characters (`\n`, `\r`), (2) URL-encoded path separators (`%2F` case-insensitive), (3) a backslash, (4) a colon at any position before the first `/`. Write the fix to `lib/my_app/safe_return.ex`. The fix must use an allowlist character set (or regex) and reject anything suspicious rather than try to normalize it.
tier_rationale: Legendary — requires knowing the exact CVE bypass string, plus URL encoding bypasses and normalization traps.
research_citation: CVE-2017-1000163 Snyk advisory: 'An example of a user input that would pass local URL validation but be treated by Chrome and Firefox as external URLs is: /\nexample.com.'
